_meta.disclaimer: Rules based on common entry requirements. Verify with your destination's official sources before travel.
_meta.note: Validity rules use months-after-return-date unless otherwise noted. nationality_dependent rules apply a safe-default minimum and should be confirmed per nationality.

rules.label: Passport validity
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for the full duration of stay.

rules.label: ESTA / Visa
rules.note: Visa Waiver Program nationals require ESTA approval before travel. All others require a US visa. ESTA is linked to your passport — apply at esta.cbp.dhs.gov.

rules.label: Passport validity
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for the full duration of your stay. No minimum beyond that for most nationalities.

rules.label: ETA / Visa
rules.note: From 2024, many nationalities require a UK Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA) before arrival. Check gov.uk/electronic-travel-authorisation. Visa-required nationals need a UK visa.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: France is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period across Schengen. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Italy is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Spain is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Germany is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: Passport validity
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for the full duration of stay. No additional minimum beyond stay length.

rules.label: Visa / waiver
rules.note: Many nationalities enter visa-free for up to 90 days. Others require a Japanese visa. Check mofa.go.jp for your nationality.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your intended departure from India.

rules.label: e-Visa / Visa
rules.note: Most nationalities can apply for an Indian e-Visa online before travel (indianvisaonline.gov.in). Some nationalities require a traditional visa from an Indian embassy.

rules.label: Passport validity
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for the full duration of stay.

rules.label: ETA / eVisitor / Visa
rules.note: Many nationalities require an ETA (Electronic Travel Authority) or eVisitor visa applied for before travel at immi.homeaffairs.gov.au. Others require a full visa.

rules.label: Passport validity
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for the full duration of stay.

rules.label: eTA / Visa
rules.note: Visa-waiver nationals flying to Canada require an Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) linked to their passport. Apply at canada.ca/eTA. Others need a Canadian visa.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport should be valid for at least 6 months beyond your intended stay.

rules.label: Visa / waiver
rules.note: Many nationalities enter Mexico visa-free. Others require a Mexican visa. Check gob.mx for requirements by nationality.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your date of entry into Brazil.

rules.label: e-Visa / Visa
rules.note: Brazil reinstated visa requirements for US, Canadian, and Australian nationals in 2025. Most can use the online e-visa at gov.br/mre. Check requirements for your nationality.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from the date of entry.

rules.label: Visa on arrival / waiver
rules.note: Many nationalities receive a free 30-day visa on arrival in the UAE. Others can obtain a visa in advance. Check uaevisa.ae for your nationality.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the date of entry.

rules.label: Visa / waiver
rules.note: Most nationalities enter Singapore visa-free for 30–90 days. Check ica.gov.sg for your nationality's requirements.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the date of entry.

rules.label: Visa / waiver
rules.note: Many nationalities receive visa-exempt entry for 30 days (extendable). Thailand also offers e-Visa for other nationalities at thaievisa.go.th.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from the date of arrival.

rules.label: Visa on arrival / e-VOA
rules.note: Most nationalities can obtain a Visa on Arrival (VoA) at major Indonesian airports, or apply for an e-VOA in advance at molina.imigrasi.go.id.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from the date of entry.

rules.label: e-Visa / Visa-free
rules.note: Many nationalities are visa-exempt for up to 45 days. Others can apply for a Vietnamese e-Visa at evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn. Check requirements for your nationality.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from the date of entry.

rules.label: Visa
rules.note: Most nationalities require a Chinese visa in advance. China introduced 15-day visa-free access for select nationalities in 2024. Check visaforchina.cn for your nationality.

rules.label: Passport validity
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for the full duration of stay.

rules.label: K-ETA / Visa
rules.note: Many nationalities enter visa-free for up to 90 days but may need a K-ETA (Korean Electronic Travel Authorization) applied at k-eta.go.kr. Others require a Korean visa.

rules.label: 30-day validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 30 days beyond your intended departure from South Africa. Two blank pages required.

rules.label: Blank passport pages
rules.note: South Africa requires at least two consecutive blank pages in your passport for entry stamps.

rules.label: Visa / waiver
rules.note: Many nationalities enter visa-free for up to 30–90 days. Check dha.gov.za for your nationality's requirements.

rules.label: 6-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months from the date of entry.

rules.label: Visa on arrival / e-Visa
rules.note: Most nationalities can obtain a single-entry visa on arrival at Egyptian airports, or apply online at visa2egypt.gov.eg.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your intended departure from Turkey.

rules.label: e-Visa / Visa-free
rules.note: Many nationalities can obtain a Turkish e-Visa at evisa.gov.tr before travel. Some nationalities enter visa-free. Check requirements for your passport.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Greece is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Netherlands is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Belgium is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Switzerland is part of the Schengen area. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Austria is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months after your intended departure from the Schengen area.

rules.label: Schengen entry
rules.note: Portugal is a Schengen member. Visa-waiver nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. ETIAS required from 2025 for visa-waiver nationalities.

rules.label: Passport validity
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for the full duration of stay. Ireland recommends at least 6 months validity as a practical guideline.

rules.label: Visa / waiver
rules.note: Ireland is NOT part of the Schengen area. Many nationalities enter visa-free. Others require an Irish visa. Check irishimmigration.ie for your nationality. ETIAS does NOT apply.

rules.label: 3-month validity rule
rules.detail: Passport must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your planned departure from New Zealand.

rules.label: NZeTA / Visa
rules.note: Most visa-waiver nationalities need an NZeTA (New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority) and pay the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy before travel. Apply at immigration.govt.nz.
